title: Kindergarten Activities
description: Growing number skills through play and discovery

level1.title: Counting & Number Names (0-20 to 100)
level1.goal: Build reliable counting, numeral recognition, and counting routines that scale to 100.

k-l1-big-idea.title: Big idea
k-l1-big-idea.content: Counting is more than reciting: children match **one number to one object**, and the *last* number answers "how many?"

k-l1-notice.title: What to notice
k-l1-notice.content:
* Touches each object once (no skipping, no double counting)
* Answers "how many?" without restarting
* Can start counting from 7 (not only from 1)
* Begins to "see" small groups quickly (1-3; then 5-frame structure)

k-l1-home.title: Try at home
k-l1-home.content:
* **Game A: "Count & Tell"** Line up 15-25 items; child counts, then you ask "How many?"
* **Game B: "Start-At Counting"** Start at 13; take turns counting until 20 (then 50).

k-l1-videos.title: Optional videos
k-l1-videos.content: **Search YouTube for:** * counting to 100 for kindergarten * one to one correspondence counting * number names 0 to 20 * 100 chart patterns kindergarten

level2.title: Compare, Order, Ten-Frames & Teen Numbers
level2.goal: Build comparison language and structured representations (ten-frames / number line), including teen numbers as 10 + ones.

k-l2-big-idea.title: Big idea
k-l2-big-idea.content: Comparing is about relationships: kids can decide "more/fewer/same" by **pairing** (not always by counting). Teen numbers are a key bridge to place value: **ten-and-some-ones**.

k-l2-notice.title: What to notice
k-l2-notice.content:
* Pairs items ("one for you, one for me")
* Recognizes ten-frame patterns without counting every dot
* Explains teen numbers ("It's 10 and 4")
* Places numbers on a 0-10 line with confidence

k-l2-home.title: Try at home
k-l2-home.content:
* **Game A: "Snack Balance"** Move snacks one at a time until bowls match.
* **Game B: "Ten-Frame Flash"** Flash a ten-frame (0-10). Say the number; then check by counting.

k-l2-videos.title: Optional videos
k-l2-videos.content: **Search YouTube for:** * ten frame kindergarten * more less same for kids * teen numbers ten and some ones * number line 0 to 20

level3.title: Make 10 & Part-Whole Thinking
level3.goal: Help children see numbers as made of parts - especially combinations that make 5 and 10.

k-l3-big-idea.title: Big idea
k-l3-big-idea.content: When kids learn numbers are **made of parts**, addition and subtraction become "rearranging parts," not memorizing. "Make 10" is a powerful bridge from counting to mental math.

k-l3-notice.title: What to notice
k-l3-notice.content:
* Names more than one way to make 6, 7, 8...
* Uses five/ten-frame structure ("5 and 2 more")
* Finds partner to 10 quickly (8 needs 2)
* Recognizes doubles (4+4) as "same + same"

k-l3-home.title: Try at home
k-l3-home.content:
* **Game A: "Ten-Frame Fill"** Put 6 pennies in a ten-frame; ask "How many more to make 10?"
* **Game B: "Shake & Spill"** Spill two-color counters; record: "__ and __ makes 10."

k-l3-videos.title: Optional videos
k-l3-videos.content: **Search YouTube for:** * make 10 kindergarten * number bonds to 10 * ten frame make 10 * subitizing to 10

level4.title: Add & Subtract Within 10 (Stories + Equations)
level4.goal: Solve add/sub problems within 10 using objects, pictures, and (lightly) equations.

k-l4-big-idea.title: Big idea
k-l4-big-idea.content: Addition and subtraction start as **real stories** (join / separate). Equations are just a way to *write down what happened*.

k-l4-notice.title: What to notice
k-l4-notice.content:
* Acts out stories before solving
* Uses fingers/objects naturally (that's fine)
* Can match a story to an equation like 4 + 2 = 6
* Solves missing-part problems with pictures

k-l4-home.title: Try at home
k-l4-home.content:
* **Game A: "Story with Toys"** "You have 4 cars, I give 2 more..." act it out; then write 4+2=__.
* **Game B: "Cover the Part"** Put 8 pennies down; cover some; ask "How many are hiding?"

k-l4-videos.title: Optional videos
k-l4-videos.content: **Search YouTube for:** * kindergarten addition subtraction within 10 * kindergarten story problems * missing addend within 10 * number bonds to 10

level5.title: Patterns, Logic, Measurement, Data & Shapes
level5.goal: Grow 'math thinking tools' (patterns, logic, planning) while exploring math in the real world.

k-l5-big-idea.title: Big idea
k-l5-big-idea.content: This is "math you can see and touch": patterns help children notice structure, and measurement/shape language helps them describe the world. Short playful talk during routines is the best practice.

k-l5-notice.title: What to notice
k-l5-notice.content:
* Explains a pattern ("it alternates")
* Tries again after feedback (no fear)
* Compares by aligning objects; uses longer/shorter/heavier/lighter
* Sorts consistently and reads a simple picture graph (most/least)
* Names shapes by attributes (corners/sides), not by orientation

k-l5-home.title: Try at home
k-l5-home.content:
* **Game A: "Clap Patterns"** Clap-tap-tap; child continues or invents a new pattern.
* **Game B: "Kitchen Shape Hunt + Graph"** Find 3 circles/3 rectangles; make a tiny picture graph and compare.

k-l5-videos.title: Optional videos
k-l5-videos.content: **Search YouTube for:** * patterns for kindergarten * coding unplugged robot directions * kindergarten measurement longer shorter * 2D and 3D shapes for kindergarten * picture graphs for kindergarten
